subject: Physics
title: Magnets: North and South
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Magnets: North and South.

hook.text: Cut a magnet in half and you get two smaller magnets—each with its own north and south pole.

buildup.text: Every magnet has two poles. Opposite poles attract; like poles repel. You cannot isolate a single pole.

discovery.text: Magnetism comes from the alignment of electron spins in atoms. When enough align, the material becomes magnetic.

twist.text: Earth itself is a giant magnet. Its magnetic field shields us from deadly solar radiation.

climax.text: A compass needle is a tiny magnet that aligns with Earth's field—pointing toward magnetic north.

punchline.text: Spinning electrons create magnetism. Earth itself is one big magnet.

quiz.question: What happens when you cut a magnet in half?
options: You get two smaller magnets, each with north and south poles
One half becomes north only and the other south only
